summary.intent: Make the gossamer indexer/poll loop more robust by relocating worktree and DB paths to inside the git repo, then extend the indexer to cover in-progress sessions from entire.io shadow branches, and fix UI regressions in the sessions table and detail views.
summary.outcome: Worktree moved to `.gossamer/checkpoints`, DB to `.gossamer/hook-handler.db`, shadow branch indexing added with 5s polling, sessions API rebuilt from `CheckpointSessionMetadata`+`ShadowSession` (dropping legacy `Session` table), and UI restored with live badges, git username, and repo name in breadcrumbs.

repo: Shadow branches follow the pattern `entire/<commit-short-hash>-<something>` and contain both `.gossamer/checkpoints/` (same as `entire/checkpoints/v1`) and `.entire/metadata/<session-id>/` for in-progress sessions.
A session transcript grows across multiple shadow branches as work progresses — the same sessionId can appear in 3+ branches with incrementally larger `full.jsonl` blobs, each with a different SHA.
The `getDb()` function in `db.ts` already calls `mkdirSync(dirname(abs), { recursive: true })` at line 51, so callers don't need to pre-create directories.

The legacy `Session` table stored `gitUserName`, `gitUserEmail`, and `repoName` populated by hook handlers; removing that table caused three UI regressions that had to be patched by reading git config at server startup.
FTS (full-text search) tables are virtual tables managed in application code, not the Prisma schema — `prisma db push` warns about them as data loss but they're safe to accept.
Git user info is reliably available via `git config user.name/email` run from the repo directory.

code.finding: `indexFullJsonl` was refactored into two functions: one taking a file path and one taking string content (`indexFullJsonlContent`), both accepting an optional `shadowSessionId` to link events to shadow sessions.
`indexAllShadowBranches` reads shadow branch content via `git ls-tree` + `git cat-file blob` — no worktree needed. Deduplication is done via a `seenBlobShas` JSON array on the `ShadowSession` record.
WS change detector must watch `logEvent` max id and `shadowSession` max id instead of the legacy `event` table to catch live session updates.
New `ShadowSession` table added with `seenBlobShas String` (JSON array) to prevent re-indexing the same content across branches. `LogEvent` gains optional `shadowSessionId` FK.
Two session endpoints (`GET /api/sessions` and `GET /api/sessions/:id`) had different indentation levels, causing `replace_all` edits to miss the second endpoint — required two separate edit calls.

workflow: When removing a git worktree registered at an old path, both `git worktree remove` and `git worktree prune` are needed to fully clean up the registration.
Deleting the SQLite DB and running `prisma db push` fresh is the cleanest recovery path when schema changes conflict with virtual FTS tables.
Reading from git refs directly (`git cat-file blob`) is a clean alternative to checking out a worktree when you only need file content from branches.

friction: Old worktree registered at `/private/tmp/gossamer-checkpoints` conflicted with the new path — required manual cleanup before the server could start.
Partial DB creation during a failed server start left a corrupt state that had to be deleted before `prisma db push` would succeed.
The same session appearing in multiple shadow branches with different blob SHAs caused duplicate indexing on first pass — required a second schema iteration to track `seenBlobShas` as a JSON array.

`replace_all` edits silently missed the second occurrence of `gitUserName: null` and `repoName: null` in `server.ts` due to different indentation in the two endpoints, requiring a follow-up fix each time.
Removing the legacy `Session` table caused three separate UI regressions (live badge, git username, repo name) that had to be discovered and fixed one by one.
open_items: The legacy `Session` table still exists in the schema but is no longer written to — it could be dropped in a future cleanup.
Shadow sessions currently show the most-recent branch name only; a full branch history per session is not exposed in the UI.
No mechanism to detect when a shadow session is 'closed' (merged into `entire/checkpoints/v1`) and transition it from `isLive: true` to a committed checkpoint in the sessions list.
Git user info is resolved once at server startup — if the git config changes (e.g. different author per repo), the server needs a restart to pick it up.
